This working sheet sketches three activation scenarios for NA / EU / APAC across our three primary cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure). Focus: align GPU provisioning cadence with model-latency SLOs, warm-pool sizing, and fallback windows to minimize user-visible regressions while containing cost.

text:
Purpose:
This working sheet sketches three activation scenarios for NA / EU / APAC across our three primary cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure). Focus: align GPU provisioning cadence with model-latency SLOs, warm-pool sizing, and fallback windows to minimize user-visible regressions while containing cost.

High-level constraints and assumptions:
- Target quarters: 2026 Q3 -> 2027 Q1.
- Primary model classes: embed-small (128 tokens), gen-medium (512 tokens), gen-large (2k tokens).
- Preferred hardware mix: A100-80GB for gen-large, A10G/RTX variants for gen-medium, T4-style for embed workload where supported.
- Cold-start penalty: 40-120ms p50 per model variant when warm-pool not present.
- Warm-pool reduces cold-starts by ~90% for pinned model variants.
- Latency SLO tiers: Platinum p95<=60ms, Gold p95<=120ms, Silver p95<=250ms.
- Cost sensitivity: keep hosted per-token marginal cost de...

Purpose and shape
---
This is a lightweight, annotated dossier: pull noisy customer fragments (support threads, pilot interviews, NPS comments) -> short inference -> concrete small experiments ("micro-probes") that eng/product can take in a 1–3 sprint cadence. Goal: convert signal fragments into something actionable, with owners and a quick effort guess. Not a canonical roadmap — think triage + experiment list.

Methodology notes
---
- Sources: eight support threads (Jan 2026), two pilot onboarding sessions, top 20 NPS verbatims, internal ops-escalation notes.
- Heuristic used: pick fragments that repeat across at least two customers or that have high operational risk (SLA, security, billing).
- Prioritization scale used here: Impact (H/M/L) x Effort (S/M/L); quick color hint in-line.

Topline synthesis (one-liners)
---
- Hosted API users report occasional large-response truncation wh...

title: Grounded Quote Skeptic: Prompt Suite + Regression Notes
preview:
Purpose:
This is a working prompt + test-case catalog for onboarding models into domain RAG flows where citation fidelity and "grounding" are critical. The focus is: when the user asks for a quote or a factual claim, the model should either (a) provide an exact quote that matches source content, with clear citation ...
text:
Purpose:
This is a working prompt + test-case catalog for onboarding models into domain RAG flows where citation fidelity and "grounding" are critical. The focus is: when the user asks for a quote or a factual claim, the model should either (a) provide an exact quote that matches source content, with clear citation metadata, or (b) refuse/clarify when the source doesn't support the claim. We stress refusal clarity for ambiguous queries and partial citations (page/paragraph level when available).

Scope and constraints:
- Domain: regulated healthcare and developer docs (FAQs, API guides).
- Sources: short snippets (paragraphs, bullet lists) plus extended docs (5-10 pages).
- Models under test: open models and tuned variants (float16, int8/quantized) across hosted and private deployments.
- Evaluation modes: automated batch scoring + manual spot checks for quote fidelity.

High-level te...

Purpose:
This working logbook is a lightweight, operational-first status tracker for customer inference rollouts where Field Ops owns day-to-day delivery. It is designed to accompany the formal implementation tracker and capture granular actions, daily standup notes, blockers and mitigation attempts. Use this during the 6-week launch window and archive a sanitized copy to Confluence after go‑live.

Scope:
- Target customers: Dedicated and Private deployment rollouts (VPCs, private clusters, reserved GPU pools)
- Phases covered: kickoff -> infra validation -> model onboarding -> canary -> traffic cutover -> stabilization
- Audience: Customer Success (Field Ops), Solutions Eng, Infra Eng, Customer SRE

How to use:
- Update entry each day with: status, owner, progress, new blockers, next actions
- Mark milestones as: Pending / In progress / Blocked / Complete

text:
Purpose:
This is a compact, engineer-friendly lab note (Flashlab Quickmap) for a 30-minute path that gets a minimal Redwood SDK call integrated into an existing app, validates latency/error modes, and wires basic telemetry and fallbacks. Goal is to have a reproducible, low-friction sequence instructors and SEs can run during demos or workshops.

When to use:
- A short intro session for a new integrator (30–45m)
- Pre-sales demo when customer wants to try a minimal flow in their environment
- Internal sandbox validation before promoting a sample to the docs site

High-level flow (30m):
1) Prep (5m): create an API key, pick a sample app folder, set env var REDWOOD_API_KEY.
2) Install (3m): pip/npm install redwood-sdk-lite (or clone example repo).
3) Hello call (5m): run single request to generate text; confirm 200 and content.
4) Add telemetry (5m): emit latency, token count metric and ...

Summary:
This working playbook captures the evaluation suite and triage process for tool-use / function-calling behaviors for onboarding models. Focus areas: tool selection accuracy, argument parsing correctness (types, units, edge values), and multi-step flows where the model must call multiple tools in sequence or call tools conditionally.

Intended audience:
- Applied ML engineers integrating new model variants into hosted/dedicated/private runs
- QA and SRE teams responsible for regression alerts
- Solutions engineering for customer-facing tool connectors

Goals:
- Provide reproducible prompt sets that exercise selection and argument extraction across typical and adversarial cases.
- Define pass/fail criteria and automated checks for CI regression gates.
- Document triage steps for systematic root-cause identification (model, prompt, runtime mapping, connector bug).

Scope and con...

Purpose:
Short working brief for a sprint of small, testable messaging + pricing experiments aimed at accelerating developer activation on the Hosted API. Executive hypothesis:
If we lead with measurable developer outcomes (time-to-first-infer, predictable latency, and quota safe-nets) and give a low-friction pricing 'first-hour' guarantee, we increase API-to-first-PoC conversion by >20% for PLG signups.

Target audiences:
- Indie devs / solo founders evaluating prototypes
- Growth-stage product engineers implementing an in-app assistant
- Platform/infra engineers validating latency/cost before procurement

Purpose and use notes:
This file is a compact, two-part working template to be used by CSMs for: (A) one-page Executive Sponsor Briefings delivered ahead of QBRs and renewal conversations, and (B) the customer-facing quarterly recap email that follows a QBR.

Quick guidance (to CSMs):
- Keep the executive briefing to one page, single-sided. Prioritize top-line business impact and a single ask.
- Use the customer-facing recap email to summarize outcomes, decisions, and agreed next steps within 72 hours of the meeting.
- Replace all {{PLACEHOLDERS}} before sending. Use metric sources (Console > Metrics > Customer) and attach the cost/latency export for dedicated customers.
- When in doubt, escalate to the AE for renewal-related asks or pricing changes.

Part A — Executive Sponsor Briefing (one pager)
Filename suggestion: exec-sponsor-briefing-{{CUSTOMER_NAME}}-Q{{QUARTER}}-{{YEAR}}.pdf

title: SDK concise pathways for non-engineer onboarding
preview:
Purpose and scope:
This is a compact, deliberately low-friction playbook for getting product managers, designers, and customer-success reps from zero to a meaningful SDK-first experiment within 30–60 minutes. Target assumes minimal terminal experience and no infra access. Focus: clarity, short happy-path, dogfooding...
text:
Purpose and scope:
This is a compact, deliberately low-friction playbook for getting product managers, designers, and customer-success reps from zero to a meaningful SDK-first experiment within 30–60 minutes. Target assumes minimal terminal experience and no infra access. Focus: clarity, short happy-path, dogfooding loop, and telemetry hooks to measure success.

Context / why this exists:
- We see non-engineering stakeholders trying SDKs and getting blocked by environment setup, secrets, or heavy examples.
- Goal: reduce cognitive load, surface value quickly, and capture metrics that tell us if the flow worked (and where).

High-level flow (30–60 min):
1) 0–5m: sign up + get a test key (sandbox) — copy/paste only, no CLI install required.
2) 5–20m: run a single small example in the browser-based notebook/gist (JS or Python).
3) 20–35m: swap in a real prompt or dataset from their produ...

title: Context window resilience matrix and prompt harness
preview:
Purpose:
This is a working report for the long-context / retrieval stress suite used during model onboarding and regression checks. Focus: context-window resilience (8k, 32k, 128k variants), prompt-placement sensitivity, truncation behavior, and attention-collapse ("attention sink") modes that cause context corrupti...
text:
Purpose:
This is a working report for the long-context / retrieval stress suite used during model onboarding and regression checks. Focus: context-window resilience (8k, 32k, 128k variants), prompt-placement sensitivity, truncation behavior, and attention-collapse ("attention sink") modes that cause context corruption or hallucination. This doc captures the harness, prompt families, experiment matrix, quick findings, and next actions.

Quick summary (TL;DR):
- Most regressions occur when retrievals are appended after a long conversational history or when the retrieval anchor is near the buffer boundary (last ~4k tokens).
- rw-infer-alpha-32k-v2 shows stable citation anchoring up to ~28k tokens but exhibits increased hallucination when the retrieved chunk overlaps a sentence boundary across the truncation point.
- rw-infer-alpha-128k-quant (int8) has lower memory bandwidth but shows an...

title: KMS implementation decision log and evidence pipeline
preview:
Purpose and scope:
This working doc captures decisions and operational notes for KMS/HSM/BYOK adoption, key rotation procedures, and how we produce tamper-evident evidence for audits and SIEM ingestion. Audience: security eng, infra, SRE, legal/compliance. Status: working draft, expect iterative updates and small ex...
text:
Purpose and scope:
This working doc captures decisions and operational notes for KMS/HSM/BYOK adoption, key rotation procedures, and how we produce tamper-evident evidence for audits and SIEM ingestion. Audience: security eng, infra, SRE, legal/compliance. Status: working draft, expect iterative updates and small experiments in the next 60 days.

High-level choices (decision log):
- Decision 2025-11-10: Use envelope encryption for all customer data at rest. Root (KEK) stored in managed HSM (cloud provider or dedicated HSM) and used only to wrap/unwrap data encryption keys (DEKs). Rationale: limits exposure of KEKs and allows lightweight rewrap on rotation.
- Decision 2025-11-18: Support BYOK for enterprise customers as a first-class flow (customer uploads key material into a dedicated HSM tenant or uses a wrapped KEK). Rationale: compliance requests and regulated customers required BY...

title: Onboarding Impact Snapshot - KV Slice Calculator
preview:

It's a lightweight 'slice' calculator to help decide whether we need additional constrained-capacity slots vs. capacity reclamation (quantization, cache tuning). Primary au...
text:
Purpose:
This working sheet estimates incremental GPU and KV memory impact for onboarding a new autoregressive chat model (vX.3) across regions. It's a lightweight 'slice' calculator to help decide whether we need additional constrained-capacity slots vs. capacity reclamation (quantization, cache tuning). Primary audience: infra, serving-runtime, and release leads.

Executive summary (short):
- Baseline fleet: 320 active A100(80GB) equivalents across NA/EMEA/APAC (effective capacity = 300 after reserved infra).
- New model vX.3 increases per-request KV by ~3.2x vs current baseline model due to longer context and wider key/value dims.
- If we keep current routing and maintain a 70% KV cache hit target, expected GPU working set increases by ~22% globally; worst-case cold-starts push demanded GPUs +42% for 24–72h after rollout without cache warming.
- Immediate actions: (1) enable target...

PURPOSE

A compact planning artifact to align the company on the next 90 days of work (30/60/90 cadence), the set of proposed company-level OKRs for exec review, and the all-hands agenda + presenter notes for the town. title: Prefetch + Prefix Collapse Sensitivity Probes (8k / 32k / 128k)
preview:
Purpose and quick summary:

This doc collects exploratory probes focused on an under-observed failure mode we started seeing in onboarding runs: \"prefix collapse\" when retrieved context is pre-fetched into KV cache and contains repeated structural tokens (TOC-like headers, repeated legal boilerplate, verbose metad...
text:
Purpose and quick summary:

This doc collects exploratory probes focused on an under-observed failure mode we started seeing in onboarding runs: \"prefix collapse\" when retrieved context is pre-fetched into KV cache and contains repeated structural tokens (TOC-like headers, repeated legal boilerplate, verbose metadata blocks). The tests also stress the interaction of prefetching and attention sink behaviour at 8k/32k/128k context sizes. Goal is to produce a small, reproducible prompt set and scoring heuristic to catch regressions in model variants and quantization profiles.

High-level hypothesis:
- When retrieval prefetch merges large structured blocks into the prefix (via automatic prefetch/caching), attention distribution can over-concentrate on repeated delimiters/headings. The runtime's attention-kernel selection + kv-cache placement causes effective token-sink behavior where th...

title: Skill-first Lateral Hire Pilot Playbook
preview:
Purpose:
This playbook defines a 12-week pilot to evaluate "skill-first" hiring for lateral senior ICs (L4-L6 engineering ICs). The pilot flips the usual resume-centric funnel: assess demonstrable day-one skills through short, paid pairing engagements and a compact on-site loop focused on practical scope and handoff...
text:
Purpose:
This playbook defines a 12-week pilot to evaluate "skill-first" hiring for lateral senior ICs (L4-L6 engineering ICs). The pilot flips the usual resume-centric funnel: assess demonstrable day-one skills through short, paid pairing engagements and a compact on-site loop focused on practical scope and handoffs. Goal = faster interviews, clearer quality signal, and a structured micro-onboarding path that maximizes early impact.

Hypothesis:
- Candidates who demonstrate productive contributions in a paid 4-8 hour pairing engagement correlate strongly with successful 30/60/90 outcomes.
- A tighter rubric that weights production-readiness and team-fit over pedigree reduces time-to-offer and lowers false positives.

Scope & constraints:
- Candidate pool: lateral ICs (3+ years experience in relevant stacks) applying to eng teams building inference, runtime, infra, and console feature...

title: Capacity drift and trigger tuning (Jan 2026)
preview:
Summary:
This sheet records the 'capacity drift' experiments run across three regions (us-west1, europe-west1, ap-southeast1) to measure how reserved GPU headroom and autoscaler triggers interact under multi-day traffic drift and burst patterns. Goal: find safe min/max reservation defaults and trigger tuning that ke...
text:
Summary:
This sheet records the 'capacity drift' experiments run across three regions (us-west1, europe-west1, ap-southeast1) to measure how reserved GPU headroom and autoscaler triggers interact under multi-day traffic drift and burst patterns. Goal: find safe min/max reservation defaults and trigger tuning that keep P95 latency within SLO and reduce reserved-GPU waste by >=12% versus current conservative defaults.

Background and motivation:
- We observed a slow upward drift in baseline token/s request load in us-west1 over the last 90 days (~7% increase), causing periodic eviction cascades when short spikes hit (chat + reranking mixes).
- Current global defaults: min=8 GPUs per pool, max=200, scale-up threshold 70% (over 2 windows), cooldown 5m. These were set conservatively and produce idle cost in low-traffic regions.
- Hypothesis: region-specific min toggles + shorter, burst-awa...

title: Regional GPU surge breakdown — weekly export
preview:

This is a weekly export meant to capture short-term surge behavior across regions and clusters, with emphasis on identifying short windows where utilization approaches provisioned capacity and recommending immediate mitigations (burst routing, temporary pool increases, and scheduled pre-warming).

Scope (th...
text:
Purpose:
This is a weekly export meant to capture short-term surge behavior across regions and clusters, with emphasis on identifying short windows where utilization approaches provisioned capacity and recommending immediate mitigations (burst routing, temporary pool increases, and scheduled pre-warming).

Scope (this export):
- Time window: rolling 7d (2025-12-29 to 2026-01-05) aggregated by day + hour-of-day patterns.
- Granularity: cluster-level and pool-level (pool = logical group of GPUs for tenancy tiers).
- Regions: us-west, us-east, eu-west, ap-south.
- Metric: GPU vRam-utilization-percent (measured per-node averaged over 1m samples), pod share, queued-requests count.

Executive summary:
- Observed 3 short-duration surges (1–3 hours) in us-west and one multi-hour sustained ramp in eu-west. Peak 7d regional averages: us-west 79%, us-east 62%, eu-west 83%, ap-south 58%.
- Top 5 ...

Context and intent:
This is a working notebook for the Juniper SDK onramp labs. Juniper is a small, opinionated JavaScript + Python client intended for rapid prototyping with low setup friction (zero config devmode, simple token + env examples).

Goals (what a dev should reach by end of 30–60m lab):
- Get a token and call the hosted Redwood API with a minimal client (JS or Python)
- Use SDK streaming and structured output to build a tiny chat widget demo
- Instrument event-level telemetry for cost/latency and a simple quality check (prompt/response hash)
- Validate local failure modes (network loss, 429s, model fallback) and a retry policy

High-level quickstart flow (30 min path):
1) prereqs: node>=18 OR python3.10, curl, basic bash
2) create api key: Account -> Tokens -> Create temporary lab token (label: juniper-lab-YYYYMMDD)
3) clone sample repo: git clone git@github.com:redwood-i...

text:
Summary:
This is a working deep-dive into a subset of onboarding evaluation failures that are low-frequency in the main metric slices but high-impact in product tests. Unlike previous clustering docs that focused on broad prompt archetypes, this note aggregates weak signals across multiple eval runs (stochastic seeds, sampling policies, decoding temperature sweep) to surface edge-case families. For each family I provide: short failure signature, representative prompts/responses, likely root cause, short-term heuristic mitigations, and medium-term engineering fixes and tests.

Context and why this matters:
- Model: open-llama-70b-quantized (candidate for hosted pipeline).
- Eval harness: onboarding-promptset-v6 (23K prompts across ecomm, search, QA, and LLM-as-code).
- Observed: global metrics passed baseline (perplexity, top-k metrics) but customer-sim tests reported 4 specific regres...

text:
Context and intent:
This is a compact, low-friction onboarding and interview-evidence bundle intended for small IC hires (engineer / infra / platform) who will be given a quiet/solo launch for their first 15 days. The goal is to reduce cognitive load, create a defensible skill signal for cross-functional partners, and accelerate contributor impact while keeping mentor time bounded. Draft for People Ops + hiring manager alignment before piloting with 2 hires.

High-level goals:
- First 15 days: remove >80% of non-essential access friction, ship 1 microtask to prod or customer-facing integration, and capture structured evidence for performance calibration.
- Mentor time budget: 3–4 hours/week active pairing + asynchronous feedback.
- Hiring alignment: ensure interview evidence maps to day-1–15 success signals so we can correlate interview rubric to ramp outcomes.

title: HITL-fallback-trigger-eval-playbook
preview:
Purpose:
This playbook defines an evaluation suite and prompt corpus for human-in-the-loop (HITL) fallback triggers in multi-step toolchains and function-calling scenarios. The goal is to a) identify when an LLM should delegate to a human, b) ensure the trigger criteria minimize false positives while catching edge-c...
text:
Purpose:
This playbook defines an evaluation suite and prompt corpus for human-in-the-loop (HITL) fallback triggers in multi-step toolchains and function-calling scenarios. The goal is to a) identify when an LLM should delegate to a human, b) ensure the trigger criteria minimize false positives while catching edge-case failures, and c) provide a regression test harness that tracks performance across model versions and runtime configs.

Scope and motivation:
- Scope: tool selection accuracy, argument parsing correctness, multi-step flow completion, and HITL fallback decision quality. Covers hosted API and Dedicated inference paths; excludes private on-prem deployment-specific network/ops constraints for now.
- Why: We continue to see subtle regressions where models either over-defer (too many unnecessary human escalations) or under-defer (miss real failure cases). Without a formalized ...

Purpose: quick decision-support for short-term hiring sprints (2–6 weeks) that balances business impact, role difficulty, and candidate fill-time. Working doc: use this during weekly recruiting syncs and interview calibration sessions.

Notes to self / context:
- Many teams need faster TTM for high-leverage ICs (infra, serving runtime, SRE). We only have limited recruiter hours and interview slots.
- Objective: create a lightweight, repeatable way to prioritize reqs and a small bank of behavioral probes that maps to our competency rubric.
- Keep interview probes short (30–90s read) so interviewers can land follow-ups rapidly.

1) Recruit Prioritization matrix (one-pager)
- Inputs (for each open req): business impact (1–5), time-to-value (weeks estimate), role difficulty (1–5), market scarcity (1–5), internal ramp cost (1–5).
- Scoring: weighted sum = (impact * 3) + (1 / time-to-value ...

Context and purpose:
This is a working doc for an experimental cross-role \"talent rotation\" hiring and onboarding pattern intended for early-stage hires and lateral ICs joining cross-functional pods. The goal is to: shorten time-to-productive, preserve hiring signal via a structured interview/debrief rubric, and validate fit quickly with a 90-day buddy/rotation sprint instead of a traditional 1:1 onboarding loop.

Who this is for:
- IC hires (SWE, ML infra, Platform, SRE, DevRel) at IC2–IC4 levels where initial scope is pod-level.
- Hiring managers who want a repeatable debrief template and scoring guidance.
- People Ops and Hiring Committee as an evidence package for fast offers.

High-level flow (timeline):
1) Screen -> take-home (optional) -> panel interviews (2 rounds) -> decision within 7 business days.
2) Offer accepted -> preboarding (one-week pre-arrival checklist).

text:
Purpose and scope:
Short experimental playbook for prioritizing telemetry signals (metrics + traces) for low-cost dashboards and higher-value trace capture. Goal is to define a lightweight, defensible approach teams can copy for resource-constrained models/tenants and for early-stage feature rollouts.

Background / problem statement:
- Query budget keeps getting eaten by open-ended trace capture and high-cardinality widgets.
- Operators need fast root-cause without blowing storage or query budgets.
- Current sampling is either too coarse (random 1%) or too greedy (capture-all on errors). We need an importance-driven sampling signal and tighter dashboard surface area.

High-level ideas (experiments to run):
1) \"Importance scoring\" per request: combine error-severity, tail-latency-percentile, model-variant-fanout, and token-economics -> produce 0..1 importance score. Use to drive trac...

text:
Purpose:
This is a lightweight, reproducible smoke plan to run during model onboarding for multilingual text generation and translation scenarios with a focus on:
- CJK (zh, ja, ko) and RTL (ar, he) language families
- Translation adequacy and fluency checks
- Locale-sensitive formatting (dates, numbers, currency, phone numbers, percent signs, punctuation)

Scope:
- Intended as the first gate after unit tests and before full-scale eval suites.
- Runs in ~1–2 minutes on a single eval worker per model variant.
- Not exhaustive — surfaced failures should trigger deeper evals (full translation suites, char-level tests, synthetic locale edgecases).

Quick summary of goals:
- Detect gross mistranslations and hallucinations for short inputs (1–3 sentence prompts).
- Confirm locale formatting behavior for common tokens (YYYY/MM/DD vs localized, thousands separators, full-width vs half-width d...

preview:

text:
Summary:
This sheet is an export of the weekly cluster/pool utilization rollup with a short drift analysis and recommended capacity moves for the next 4 weeks. Focus is on NA/EU regional pools that show steady growth in steady-state production traffic and a new recurring spike in experimental batch jobs (tracked INF-748).

Key takeaways (TL;DR):
- Fleet-wide average GPU util (7d): 64% avg, 92% 95th percentile across peak windows.
- NA-west/generic-pool shows upward drift (+8 pp week-over-week) driven by two long-running batched eval jobs.
- EU-central/dedicated-low-latency has headroom but increasing tail latency for seq_len > 1024.
- Recommend short-term reservation: +12x A100-equivalent in NA-west for next 14 days; longer term consider autoscale policy adjustment for experimental batch label.

Detailed per-cluster rollup (most recent 7d window):
Cluster,Region,Pool,GPUs,total_hours_...

Purpose and scope:
This is a lightweight, reusable kit for running a 72-hour practical assessment across roles (SWE/backend, ML engineer/infra, product manager, SRE/ops). The goal is to: 1) get a sense for hands-on problem solving and communication, 2) reduce onsite time by pushing practicals earlier, 3) provide consistent scoring and a minimal day-zero checklist so accepted candidates can be productive on day 1.

Intended use and constraints:
- Use for mid-to-senior IC hires (IC3-IC5).
- Volunteer-based practicals: candidate chooses to accept practical phase after initial screen.
- Should take ~6–10 hours total for candidate over 72 hours (split tasks + writeups).
- Not a take-home whiteboard replacement — emphasize realistic artifacts (small PR, infra terraform snippet, decision memo, runbook).

text:
Goal: single-developer can go from checkout -> working sampleapp -> instrumented smoke run in ~15 minutes. This is intentionally compact: surface only the must-do steps, fail-safe fallbacks, and a tiny set of prompts+telemetry keys.

Context and constraints:
- Host: Redwood hosted API + local stub runner for offline workflows.
- Languages: Node + Python minimal paths.
- Assumes dev has API key and basic git/ssh setup.
- Must include a deterministic smoke test (latency + token-cost check) and an example failure fallback (model downgrade).

Runbook (15-min split):
1) 0:00-2:00 - bootstrap repo
 - git clone <repo> ; cd quick-infra-sample
 - npm i || pip install -r requirements.txt
 - copy .env.example -> .env and paste API_KEY (note: mention secrets manager later)
 - quick sanity: node ./scripts/hello-redwood.js (or python run/hello.py) -> expect status 200

2) 2:00-6:00 - wire a sample ...

title: Latency-aware GPU SKU routing and procurement notes
preview:

Quick working notes tying regional latency requirements and customer SLAs to SKU-level procurement decisions and routing policies. Trying to make procurement decisions more directly informed by the product-side latency sensitivity map so we avoid buying excess high-cost SKUs for low-latency-inflexible workl...
text:
Purpose:
Quick working notes tying regional latency requirements and customer SLAs to SKU-level procurement decisions and routing policies. Trying to make procurement decisions more directly informed by the product-side latency sensitivity map so we avoid buying excess high-cost SKUs for low-latency-inflexible workloads and conversely avoid under-provisioning latency-critical customers.

Context / problem statement:
- We have heterogeneous SKU inventory across regions (A100-40GB, H100, L40, T4-refurb).
- Different customer routes have different latency targets: interactive-chat (p95 < 120ms), async-batch-embed (p95 < 2000ms), realtime-llm (p95 < 80ms).
- Procurement lead times vary by SKU and region (H100 orders: 12-18w; A100: 8-12w; L40: 6-10w; refurbished T4: 2-4w).
- Current procurement cadence is driven by burn-rate smoothing and price windows, not latency class needs, which creat...

title: Sidecar LLM Adapter Onboarding Sprint Plan (pilot)
preview:
Purpose:
A compact sprint plan and quickstart outline for the Sidecar LLM Adapter pilot. Goal is to validate a 'sidecar' integration pattern for customer SDKs that keeps model serving concerns separate from app developers' runtime and reduces friction when upgrading runtime or switching deployment modes (hosted/dedi...
text:
Purpose:
A compact sprint plan and quickstart outline for the Sidecar LLM Adapter pilot. Goal is to validate a 'sidecar' integration pattern for customer SDKs that keeps model serving concerns separate from app developers' runtime and reduces friction when upgrading runtime or switching deployment modes (hosted/dedicated/private).

Background and rationale:
- Customers ask for minimal code changes to adopt Redwood APIs; sidecar pattern can provide a local HTTP/gRPC adapter that exposes simple SDK-compatible endpoints while proxying/optimizing to Redwood runtime.
- Allows teams to ship faster, keep tokens/telemetry consistent, and support feature toggles (caching, batching, fallbacks) in the sidecar rather than app code.

Sprint goals (4 weeks):
1) MVP sidecar binary + container image: config-driven, minimal footprint.
2) Quickstart docs and one-page runbook for developers + example ap...

text:
Purpose:
This playbook provides a compact, asynchronous-first onboarding and handoff template for hires that will operate in a hybrid model (remote + periodic in-office). It is optimized for fast ramp and transfer of domain knowledge for contract-to-perm and cross-functional lateral moves. The document is intentionally checklist-driven so hiring managers, mentors, and new hires can run a 10-day rapid activation followed by a structured 30–90 day growth ladder with clear success signals.

Success criteria (example):
- New hire can independently complete the basic support flow / ship a small scoped change / run the weekly oncall runbook by day 30.
- Positive qualitative mentor feedback by week 6.
- Role-readiness score >= 80% on the role-competency checklist by day 90.

Quick notes on behavior and assumptions:
- Async-first: most prep, knowledge transfer, and checks are written or recor...
